official_sources.notes: Chandra OCR 2 repository, commercial usage, benchmark, and model license summary.
Chandra OCR 2 model card with OpenRAIL label, usage, and benchmark scores.
PaddleOCR repository, Apache 2.0 license, document parsing claims, and release status.
olmOCR toolkit, Apache 2.0 license, benchmark table, and remote/local inference options.
Docling document conversion toolkit, MIT license, OCR/layout support, and CLI integration.
Granite Docling model documentation, Apache 2.0 license, enterprise document conversion fit.
Tesseract OCR engine, Apache 2.0 license, and integration baseline.
OCRmyPDF, MPL 2.0 license, Tesseract-backed searchable PDF OCR workflow.

fixture_set.description: Digital PDF company introduction with headings, product claims, and mixed visual hierarchy.
Pitch or sales deck PDF with sparse slides, charts, logos, and image captions.
Long-form report with multi-column text, tables, footnotes, and appendix content.
Scanned or photographed PDF page set with skew, noise, and imperfect contrast.
Korean/English mixed company profile with tables and Korean section labels.

commercial_restrictions: free for research and personal use
free for startups under USD 2M funding or revenue
cannot be used competitively with the hosted API
broader commercial use requires separate licensing

candidate_tools.name: PaddleOCR / PaddleOCR-VL / PP-Structure
observed_strengths: structured Markdown and JSON document parsing
resource-efficient OCR and document parsing paths
candidate_tools.reason_not_selected: permissive and practical, but no verified head-to-head evidence in this review proves better OCR/layout performance than Chandra 2 for our fixture mix

candidate_tools.name: Docling + Granite Docling
candidate_tools.license: MIT codebase plus Apache-2.0 Granite Docling model
candidate_tools.reason_not_selected: license and integration fit are strong, but performance still needs our own Korean/company-introduction and table-heavy benchmark

candidate_tools.name: olmOCR 2
observed_strengths: local and OpenAI-compatible remote inference options
candidate_tools.reason_not_selected: public benchmark scores reviewed here are below Chandra 2 overall

candidate_tools.name: Tesseract + OCRmyPDF
observed_strengths: good baseline for token savings on scanned PDFs
candidate_tools.reason_not_selected: not expected to match Chandra 2 on complex tables, layout, handwriting, or document understanding

candidate_tools.name: Unstructured local partitioning
observed_strengths: partitioning APIs for PDF and office formats
candidate_tools.reason_not_selected: license fit is promising but OCR/layout performance must be measured on our fixtures

candidate_may_be_selected_only_if: direct_chandra_2_superiority_proven is true on project fixtures or accepted public head-to-head benchmark
DeckIntake JSON validity does not regress
token savings are material without reducing intake quality
CLI/MCP integration does not bypass consent, support-bundle, or local-only policies
selection_rules.why_no_replacement_selected: No reviewed alternative currently proves both a more permissive commercial license and better OCR/layout performance than Chandra 2 for the intended large PDF/company-introduction to DeckIntake workload.

pipeline_options_to_measure.description: OCR/layout parser -> compact Markdown/JSON -> LLM extraction -> DeckIntake
Parser JSON -> deterministic field mapping -> LLM fills missing DeckIntake fields
Claude/Codex/Gemini direct document reading where permitted by explicit consent

deck_intake_projection_sample.name: Document RAG Intake Evaluation Sample
deck_intake_projection_sample.primary_goal: Summarize a source company introduction into a concise decision-ready deck plan.

source_materials.description: Redacted parser output summary, not the raw source document.

deck_intake_projection_sample.notes: Evaluation fixture only; not a shipped document/RAG intake feature.
